# Questions in Livestream 67

Live Stream #67: Ignorance of the Incredulously Fallacious.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=285" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:45</a> | I have heard a North Semitic form of Yahweh means "he blows" and points to Yahweh as a storm deity. What do you think of that view? | He is not persuaded. He thinks the linguistic development is weak, prefers a Midian-related origin for the name, and does not see good evidence that Yahweh began as a Levantine storm deity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=516" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:36</a> | How would you address the problem of evil? | He says there is not just one problem of evil, and that evil is a problem for atheists as well as theists. He treats it as mainly a philosophy-of-religion issue rather than his main field. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=750" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:30</a> | Does the supposed contradiction between 2 Samuel 21:19 and 1 Chronicles 20:5 affect biblical inspiration? | He does not think it is a contradiction. He argues the later Goliath reference can be a reused family or clan name rather than the same individual David killed earlier. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=1372" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:52</a> | Does Jude 6-7 say angels committed sexual immorality? | No. He argues the grammar points back to Sodom, Gomorrah, and the surrounding cities, not to the angels, and reads the passage as parallel examples of judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=1885" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:25</a> | Are you going to watch *The Rings of Power*? What are your thoughts going in? | He is very negative about it, says he might watch it later, and expects it to miss Tolkien badly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=2034" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:54</a> | Is there any ancient Near Eastern prophetic literature outside the Bible that uses amphibole or double meanings, and are there sources to read? | Yes. He says Egyptian prophetic literature shows this, gives examples such as *Neferti* and *Admonitions of Ipuwer*, but does not name a specific go-to study. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=2315" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:35</a> | In Revelation 11:15, is "the kingdom of this world" singular, and if so what kingdom does it mean? | He does not take it as the Antichrist's kingdom in particular. He reads it as the fallen created order becoming Christ's domain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=2528" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:08</a> | What were the Amara West temple inscriptions for, and why are Seir and the Shasu there in a Nubian temple? | He says the temple functioned as a boundary-hegemony monument. The inscription sequence helps place the Shasu of Yhw near Seir and Edom, which is why he thinks the text matters geographically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=3173" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:53</a> | Is there evidence Moses or Egypt conquered Ethiopia before the exodus, as Josephus and some films suggest? | No. He says Egypt conquered Nubia, not Ethiopia proper, and he sees no evidence Moses was a military commander; Josephus is not a reliable source here. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=3875" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:35</a> | If someone has less than four hours a week, what is the best way to become as biblically literate as possible? | He recommends daily reading rather than one weekly block: roughly 30 to 45 minutes a day in Scripture plus scholars, including writers one agrees and disagrees with. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=4122" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:42</a> | When Paul sounds like Plato, is he deliberately referencing him, and was Plato inspired by God? | He allows that Paul may echo pagan thinkers, but says Plato was not inspired in the biblical sense. Instead, pagan writers can contain partial truths that inspired biblical authors may use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=4628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:08</a> | Does Petrovich know what he is talking about in that interview with Costa? | He thinks the interview was a train wreck and says the claims there were badly handled. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=4907" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:47</a> | Did Egyptians really come from the land of Punt? | No. He says Egyptians were a blended population shaped by North Africa and the Levant, with some southern influence, not a people simply originating from Punt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=5155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:55</a> | Does the biblical prohibition on sorcery include narcotics? | In some cases, yes. He says the issue is not narcotics in themselves but using drugs for forbidden religious or sorcerous purposes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=5396" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:56</a> | Is there merit in good attempts at comparative religion? | Yes. He sees comparative religion as useful for tracing similarities, contrasts, and context, but says it goes wrong when similarity is inflated into borrowing or identity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=5712" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:12</a> | Is the Peshitta reflective of Yeshua's native language? | Only partly. He thinks Jesus likely lived in a multilingual environment with Aramaic, Hebrew, Greek, and probably some Latin in play, so the question is broader than one native language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=6156" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:36</a> | Should Christians try to reconstruct a proto-faith or original early form of Christianity? | No, not as a practical goal. He thinks that can ignore later theological clarification and the legitimate diversity of Christian expression across cultures and history. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=6552" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:12</a> | Is the Soleb inscription the oldest Yahweh inscription, and what does it do to exodus dating? | He says the inscription is important but too vague to settle much. He does not think it affects exodus dating and does not identify the Shasu there with Israel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=6938" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:38</a> | Were there Egyptian colonies in China? | He doubts it. He allows long-distance trade between Egypt and the far east, but says that is not the same as proving Egyptian colonies in China. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=7371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:51</a> | Do we have any archaeological report identifying who the Rephaim were? | He says not much. They are one of the groups we know very little about, and he is not aware of a firm archaeological identification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=7649" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:29</a> | What is going on in Leviticus 25:44-46? | He reads the passage as distinguishing several slave categories: already-enslaved foreigners, resident-alien families in debt, and indebted Israelites, with different rules applying to each. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=8414" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:14</a> | Is it necessary for Scripture to be inerrant? Can it be authoritative and errant? | He says the copies we have can contain copying mistakes and still be authoritative, while classical inerrancy refers to the original autographs. Practically, he still thinks the text is highly trustworthy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=9039" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:39</a> | If you could go back to the exodus, what would be the first thing you would ask Moses? | He says he would ask what life was like in the palace at Avaris and who Moses' adoptive mother was. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=9190" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:10</a> | In your opinion, where do you place Mount Sinai? | He places it somewhere in the Sinai Peninsula, probably in the central region, but says the exact mountain cannot be known with confidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=9350" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:50</a> | Did you get a chance to read the recent BASOR article on the Khirbet Qeiyafa ostracon? | No. He says he had not read it yet and asked for the question to be raised again after he had time to review the article. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=9701" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:41</a> | Can you explain Exodus 4:24-25, the circumcision fiasco? | He says Moses had failed to apply covenant circumcision to his son, creating a serious problem for his role as God's representative. Zipporah acts in his place and saves his life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=10182" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:42</a> | Did Egyptians believe in curses surrounding pharaohs' tombs? | To an extent, yes. He says curses are more about defilement, tomb robbery, or entering while ritually impure than about ordinary respectful entry into tomb complexes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=10498" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:58</a> | Is the drawing on the ostracon or "umbrella" article the earliest depiction of Moses yet found? | He does not know yet. He had not read the article and deferred the question until he could research it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N3rD-n6CbYY?t=10592" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:32</a> | Are Egyptian obelisks like Min symbols, and did the Israelites build them? | He says no on both counts. He treats obelisks as solar symbols, not phallic Min symbols, and says Israelites are not known to have built them. |
